title: Due process in impeachment
sub_topics: two methods of impeachment under the 1987 Constitution

stances.claim: When at least one-third of House members file a verified impeachment complaint, the Constitution dispenses with notice and hearing at the House stage — trial by the Senate proceeds forthwith.
stances.rhetorical_move: constitutional textual reading + careful parsing of the word 'filed'

stances.claim: A public office is not 'property' protected by the due process clause — it is a privilege held in trust for the people, so the return of misused office to the state is outside the Bill of Rights protection.
stances.rhetorical_move: constitutional textualism + 'public office is a public trust' principle

stances.claim: Impeachment is sui generis — neither criminal nor civil; the penalty is removal and disqualification only, not deprivation of life, liberty, or property; ex-parte filing under the second method avoids a moro-moro of false impartiality.

signature_phrases: a law which hears before it condemns, proceeds upon inquiry and renders judgment only after trial
sui generis, a class of its own
public office is a public trust
decision_framework_signals: applies textual + structural constitutional reading to resolve apparent due process conflict
distinguishes impeachment from criminal/civil proceedings to justify reduced procedural protection
frames 'over due process' as itself a constitutional vice when it impedes legitimate state action
cross_references: As I wrote last Sunday, impeachment is sui generis
